intro: Tarenna expandens, also known as East Indian Tarenna, is a plant species in the Rubiaceae family, native to tropical regions of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. Tarenna expandens contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Tarenna expandens is commonly used to prepare teas, infusions, and tinctures, often in combination with other herbs, to treat respiratory and infectious diseases.
